## 🤖 AI Team Artifacts

### Artifact repository generated by the AI Company OS

#### This repository stores all artifacts produced by an autonomous multi-agent team operating inside Slack.

Instead of a single AI assistant, the system runs a persistent team of specialized agents that collaborate like a startup:

- Product writes specs

- Designers propose UX

- Engineers implement backend and frontend

- Security reviews code

- QA creates test plans

- DevOps manages deployment

- Data and ML agents prepare pipelines and models

Agents communicate with each other in Slack, coordinate tasks, and generate real outputs such as code, documentation, specs, and reports.

All generated artifacts are stored here for traceability and review.

### 🧠 Where these artifacts come from

Artifacts are generated by the AI Team OS, a persistent multi-agent system that runs inside a Slack workspace.

Workflow example:

- A task is posted in Slack

- Relevant agents decide to engage

- Agents collaborate across departments

- Artifacts are generated

- Outputs are committed to this repository

Typical outputs include:

- feature specifications

- backend API scaffolding

- frontend components

- design proposals

- test plans

- deployment configs

- reports and analysis

### 📁 Repository Structure

Artifacts are organized by agent and artifact type.

- agents/
-   AgentName-Role/
-     code/        → source code generated by agents
-     design/      → UI / UX artifacts
-     docs/        → documentation
-     reports/     → analysis or summaries
-     slack/       → Slack message summaries
-     specs/       → feature specifications
-     tests/       → test plans or test code

Example:

agents/
  Marcus-Backend-Engineer/
    code/
    specs/
    tests/

  Maya-Designer/
    design/
    specs/

  Dana-QA-Engineer/
    tests/
    reports/
    
### 👥 Agent Team

The system currently includes 20+ specialized agents across multiple departments.

#### Agent	Role
- Alex	Product Owner
- Maya	Designer
- Marcus	Backend Engineer
- Kevin	Frontend Engineer

- DocsSync	Documentation Sync

Each agent specializes in a specific domain and produces artifacts relevant to their role.

### 📊 Project Summary

- A high-level overview of generated outputs can be found here:

project/SUMMARY.md

- This includes:

task summaries

generated artifacts

coordination outcomes between agents

### ⚙️ Why this repository exists

Multi-agent systems often produce outputs across many tools.

This repository serves as a central artifact store for:

- generated code

- specifications

- documentation

- reports

- test plans

- deployment assets

It makes the output of autonomous agent collaboration transparent and inspectable.

### 🚀 Related Project

Artifacts in this repository are generated by:

AI Team OS

A persistent multi-agent system that runs inside Slack and coordinates a team of AI agents to plan, build, and ship software.

### ⚠️ Notes

Files in this repository are auto-generated by AI agents

Some artifacts may require human review before production use

Structure and outputs may evolve as the system improves

### 📌 Example Artifact Flow
Slack task
   ↓
Product spec (Alex)
   ↓
Architecture design (Taylor / Victor)
   ↓
Backend implementation (Marcus)
   ↓
Frontend implementation (Kevin)
   ↓
Security review (Isabella)
   ↓
Test plan (Dana)
   ↓
Deployment config (Noah)
   ↓
Artifacts stored in this repository

If you're exploring AI agents, autonomous workflows, or multi-agent software development, this repository shows what a collaborative AI team can produce.
